quote.text: It may be observed in general that the future is purchased by the present.
quote.author: Samuel Johnson, The Rambler No. 175 (19 November 1751)

macro.title: What It Means For VWRA, Global Bonds, Gold & BTC

stories.kicker: The World · The Gulf War, Day 29
stories.headline: The Houthi Blockade of Saudi Arabia Becomes Real
stories.dek: A declaration made last weekend hardened this week into convoy escorts and threatened shipping, as the Gulf war kept narrowing onto the two straits it cannot stop returning to.

stories.headline: For a Few Hours, the Biggest Company Made Phones
stories.dek: Apple briefly retook the market-cap crown from Nvidia as the AI-chip trade wobbled; the tungsten-gas squeeze found a price; and the next Nvidia platform entered full production even as its China sales stayed near zero.

stories.headline: Half a Billion Views for a Film Ten Months Away
stories.dek: The first Avengers: Doomsday trailer set a record the week before Comic-Con — where Marvel returns to Hall H and Apple TV+ arrives for the first time.

stories.headline: A New Chief Justice, Named Nineteen Months Ahead
stories.dek: Sushil Nair will succeed Sundaresh Menon in February 2027 — a long-lead succession announced the same week the government closed the book on a resigned minister's seat.

stories.headline: Bedok's Record Bid, Now Confirmed
stories.dek: A Frasers-led consortium's S$2.13 billion offer for the Bayshore Drive site is the top bid at tender close — Singapore's largest non-central land sale — while Turf City's first private condo opens its books this weekend.

stories.headline: Unpacked Comes to London: A Wider Fold, and Glasses With No Screen
stories.dek: Samsung held its first London Unpacked in fourteen years on 22 July, unveiling a reshaped foldable line, its first commercial AI glasses — and a set of prices that keep climbing.

stories.headline: The Spec Runs Ahead Again
stories.dek: Home Assistant's July release finally rebuilt its Matter server just as Matter itself jumped to 1.6 — NFC-tap setup, cross-ecosystem sharing, smarter thermostats. The migrator's takeaway: adopt slowly.

stories.headline: A Flagship Season for the Ear
stories.dek: From a $399 headphone to a $210,000 speaker, the summer's audio launches span the whole ladder at once — while the auction market that sets watch prices just posted its strongest half in years.

stories.headline: The Paediatricians Drop the Clock
stories.dek: New guidance retires the decade-old "hours per day" rule for screens in favour of content, context and the individual child — a quieter, harder message than a number. General guidance, not medical advice.

stories.headline: An oil bid, a coin-flip Fed, and gold at a record
stories.dek: A widening Gulf war keeps a bid under crude just as a hawkish chair drags July's meeting toward a live call; gold prints a record while bitcoin only nudges a five-week high. Market explanation and education, not investment advice — forecasts are flagged as forecasts throughout.

stories.headline: The Third Visitor From Another Star
stories.dek: An interstellar comet is crossing our sky this week, the gravitational-wave catalogue just passed 390, and NTU found exotic light hiding in a 200-year-old shadow.

stories.headline: Feeling Loved Beats Being Loving
stories.dek: A new study separates two things we usually blur — and finds that the sense of being loved does more for a relationship than the act of loving. Meanwhile, three in four online daters say they would date a machine.

stories.headline: The Strength Dose That Comes With a Ceiling
stories.dek: A large study points to 90–120 minutes of strength training a week as the sweet spot — with no bonus above it — and a wearable head-to-head suggests which recovery number to trust. General guidance, not medical advice.

stories.headline: The Autumn Squeeze
stories.dek: Three arena-sized names land within a month of each other this autumn — book the travel before the tickets.

stories.headline: Pricing the Unarrived
stories.dek: Half a billion trailer views, a Fed meeting not yet sat, a bid on empty land, a war premium on oil still flowing: this week was thick with prices for things that have not happened. Three episodes — a rice market, a bubble and a mania — about what a society is really doing when it buys the future.
